content_preview.title: Visa Launches Six AI Tools to Overhaul Credit Card Dispute Resolution as Global Volumes Surpass 106 Million
content_preview.summary: Visa has unveiled a suite of six AI-powered services targeting the credit card dispute process, deploying generative AI for automated merchant representment and predictive models for issuer decision support as global dispute volumes reach 106 million annually, up 35 percent since 2019.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Visa announced six new artificial intelligence tools on April 1 designed to modernize what the payments giant called an "outdated" credit card dispute process, targeting a system that handled more than 106 million disputes globally in 2025 — a 35 percent increase since 2019, according to [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/01/visa-ai-tools-dispute-management.html).

The tools split evenly between merchant-facing and issuer-facing solutions, combining generative AI for automated responses with pr...

editor_notes.content_quality: Good AI applications article covering Visa's dispute resolution tools.
editor_notes.source_verification: CNBC: 403 paywall. BusinessWire: timed out during manual fetch but verified reachable by automated check. Both are reputable sources (CNBC on allowlist, BusinessWire is standard press release platform). Automated source checks passed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Claims sourced from CNBC and BusinessWire press release — both established outlets.
concerns: Manual source verification limited by timeouts/paywalls, but automated checks passed
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid AI applications article. Approved based on automated checks and source credibility.
